title: Scaling social science research
summary: Introduces GABRIEL, an open-source toolkit using GPT to convert qualitative text and images into quantitative data, letting social scientists analyze research material at scale.

title: GPT-5.3-Codex System Card
summary: System card for GPT-5.3-Codex, an agentic coding model that combines GPT-5.2-Codex's frontier coding performance with GPT-5.2's reasoning and professional-knowledge capabilities.

title: GPT-5 lowers the cost of cell-free protein synthesis
summary: Blog reporting an autonomous lab that combined GPT-5 with Ginkgo Bioworks' cloud automation to cut cell-free protein synthesis costs by 40% through closed-loop experimentation.

summary: Reports the second phase of Project Vend, Anthropic's experiment running a small in-office shop staffed by an AI 'shopkeeper' (Claude), examining how the system handles complex, open-ended real-world business operations

title: Evaluating AI’s ability to perform scientific research tasks
summary: Presents FrontierScience, a benchmark of research-level physics, chemistry, and biology problems designed to measure AI reasoning progress toward genuine scientific research tasks rather than textbook recall.

title: Advancing science and math with GPT-5.2

title: How confessions can keep language models honest

title: How AI is transforming work at Anthropic
summary: Combines surveys, qualitative interviews, and internal Claude Code usage data to characterize how AI tools are reshaping engineering and research workflows at Anthropic.

title: Estimating AI productivity gains from Claude conversations
summary: Analyzes roughly 100,000 Claude conversations to estimate task-level productivity effects, finding AI assistance reduces task completion time by about 80% on average.

title: How evals drive the next chapter in AI for businesses
summary: OpenAI argues that rigorous evaluations let businesses define, measure, and improve AI system performance, framing evals as a tool to reduce deployment risk, boost productivity, and gain strategic advantage.

title: GPT-5.1-Codex-Max System Card

title: Understanding neural networks through sparse circuits
summary: OpenAI blog describing its sparse-circuit approach to mechanistic interpretability-training weight-sparse models with disentangled, human-readable circuits-as a path toward more transparent and reliable AI behavior.

title: Project Fetch: Can Claude train a robot dog?

title: Sora 2 is here
summary: Announces Sora 2, a video generation model that is more physically accurate, realistic, and controllable than prior systems, adding synchronized dialogue and sound effects, available in the new Sora app.
